prompt: Use the uploaded robot image as a complete reference source. Maintain the head shape, large glowing compound eyes, dark blue and gold color scheme, and retro-futuristic design while deforming it into a 1970s Japanese soft vinyl (sofubi) toy. The theme is 'a used sofubi released in 1978 that has been played with by children for decades.' On the left side of the screen is the original box. It is heavily sun-bleached, with crushed corners and tears repaired with cellophane tape. The printing is rubbed and fading, with price tags and remnants of stickers applied by children. There is also deformation and dirt from moisture. Not a leftover from a Showa-era toy store, but an atmosphere of having been actually stored at home for decades. On the right side of the screen is the robot sofubi figure. The head and shoulders are covered in small dents from being dropped on the floor many times. The paint on the nose and horns is peeling, exposing the base sofubi material color. There are fine scratches on the compound eye parts. Countless scratches on the arms and legs from play. The soles of the feet are worn and rounded. The dark blue vinyl has partially faded from years of sunlight. The gold paint is more than half peeled off, with the coating cracking. Accumulation of grime and dust around the joints. Overall decrease in gloss due to aging. No new glossy feel. Faint traces of a name written by a child with an oil-based pen. Rubbed marks from trying to erase graffiti. Small cracks repaired with glue. Realistic sense of life from a Showa-era child playing seriously. On a wooden study desk. The desk has fine scratches and pencil marks. The background is a blurred scene reminiscent of a Showa-era child's room. Warm incandescent light bulb lighting. Evening atmosphere. A photorealistic product photo as if taken of an actual used sofubi found by a toy collector. Film-style graininess. Minute dust. High definition. Vintage toy magazine feature page quality. extremely worn vintage sofubi, heavily played with by children, chipped paint, scratches, dents, faded colors, repaired cracks, dust accumulation, realistic aging, authentic Japanese vintage toy photography, photorealistic, collectible toy documentation no mint condition, no factory new appearance, no modern collectible figure, no premium statue, no pristine surface, no clean packaging, no modern toy store display
